transcript.text: Hey.
What's up?
Nice to meet you in person or well over Zoom.
Yeah.
Nice to meet you too.
Hi, Marcel.
Hi, two of my favorite humans on this company in the same call.
Awesome.
How's it going?
Good.
Okay, cool.
I know we don't have a ton of time, so I'll get to it.
Nurse already has a lot of the context, so Will's more about like bringing you up to speed and kind of understanding a little bit where my head is at.
And so as the thing that driving the.

The urgency is the fact that on the delivery side, I've recorded so many things and there's like so many things I wrote over the last year or so that were just like very much spot on, on like the intent of how we wanted people to operate and do.

And, and I think over time somehow like things just got like archived and lost and, and, you know, I'm trying to make sure we kind of revive what is truly the source of truth, you know, and then recently on top of all of that, there's also a ton of recordings that we've done together that you're aware of that Marisol might not have the full view yet because it's not live yet.
or if it is like, you know, she wasn't in the weeds knowing that it was happening.
And, and so, um, we asked Marisol to, uh, help us pull together all the, the knowledge and training and things like that, mostly.
With the intent of like, how do you go from a brand new client signing a contract to successfully converting them to a 12 month contract?
But in the context of that, you don't need to do a good job.
You also need the whole company context as well, right?
So that's where like the two kind of like merge.
And then to bring it kind of like full picture, the goal is eventually like a lot of the training, a lot of the things here are things that are in the community.

And hopefully we have more people like William, which was like, you know, consuming all the stuff and then he comes on and within the first week is already way more up to speed than most people would be because he's already consumed like five or six other workshops and things like that.
And so like the goal is like before we kind of like set up the perfect curriculum or whatever, it's like inventorying everything and putting it in one place.
I'm thinking through and trying to find where the stuff is that's like written.
And then.
Things like that, but I think like a lot of it lives in video, right?
And there's, like, I'll give you an example.
There's this one video that Bridget and Tucker asked for you to edit, and I don't think anyone's using it.
And I'm like, but then I keep getting notifications of people watching this old loom that is linked to from, like, all our job applications.
And I'm like, well, there's this other one that's way better, you know?
And so, like, that's a lot of what's happening.
And it's just, like, the better version is not being brought up and knowledge is too spread out.
This is kind of, like, not meant to be a, like, okay, let's boil the ocean here.
Let's, like, get everything perfect.
mostly, like, can we put everything in one place?
Can we make sure everything is transcribed?
Can we make sure everything has, like, a good title or something?
And then from there, we kind of have better awareness of, like, what we currently have and then match it against, like, but we need to have it.
like...
See where the gaps are, right?
Marcel, from your context, did I miss anything?
Nope.
You summarized it, basically.
Cool.
And there's more than the video.
I know that.
It's just like the video, I think it's like a good starting point.
And then, Marcel, I think on your end, the area where you could help Will is also on the calls.
I'll give you an example.
We started Strategy Sprint June 1st.
Argument was the very first client we did in the Strategy Sprint with.
You were involved from very early on.
I can't remember if it was the first week or early-ish, right?
And so there's a lot of things there.
And Tyler also probably has videos of me on sales calls and things like that, right?
there's, I think, a lot of good examples.
But we can kind of curate from like Fathom and whatnot and then extract and then give it to Will to, you know, chop up a little bit.
Very, very basic, right?
Will is just like one angle only.
So it's just mostly like get to the right thing and then uploading a better like version to this inventory.
Think of it as like examples.
One is like me talking, us like sharing bigger context and whatnot or individual workshops for trainings and things like that.
The other one is more like examples and actions that will help, you know.
That makes perfect sense to me.
And I think between those two, like I think we'll cover a lot of ground just because we have literally like thousands of calls.
So we don't need to go through thousands of calls, but I think we can, we know the engagements that are going really well.
And we know like, you know, we can pull like, for example, Marisol, like a couple of deep dives, right?
Like from a couple of different.
We can pull a couple of kickoff calls and pretty quickly pull the ones that I'm involved in and things like that.
And then almost week to week, maybe we can have a good example of a handoff.
And if truly there's no good example we can find where we're all like, oh, no, this is really good, then we know that it's like, okay, this is what we need to go do and make sure we record as well.
Yeah, makes perfect sense to me.

And I'm already kind of like on the same wavelength, dig, released, or send me some recordings that I'm downloading like little snippets of, and I'll pull them into a timeline and try to make it into one video.
But everything I'll do, I'll run past you, Marisol, and you can kind of be, you know, help me kind of say like on message, because you have a little bit more context.
And then any other videos that you have, I know you already sent a few.
But yeah, this is going to be working.
Work progress probably for a couple days, but no problem, Marcel, we can get this done.
Cool.
Sounds good.

There are some as well, Will, like, for instance, like, when we talked about, like, context, and then I think with Cece on the webinar, it was like, oh, I really like how you thought about this, like, that snippet, for example, right?
Like, we might be able to pull out some of those, where it's just like, no, no, this really focuses on something that is a really important way to think about it, you know?
Same thing with, like, the whiteboarding session.
The whiteboarding session that I did is an hour and a half, but it's like, to me, that's like one of the first things people need to watch as well.
But maybe there's, like, ways to decompose it out.
Not everyone's going to have an hour and a half, you know?
And I think, like, those, like, the ones that kind of set the context, I think, are really, really important to kind of, like, have it, like, up front, you know?
Again, like, with the...
It doesn't need to be like a bunch of different angles or whatnot.
This is truly just like, let's just get the core points across and decompose it into chapters so people understand what the chapters are in there.
And my guess is I think we already have easily 30 hours of footage that is like between everything we've ever had, you know.
So for me, like if we get it to like, what is the 20 hours that people should watch and pay attention to?
And then for each one of those, come up with like a good like cloud project and prompts and things like that to just like kind of process it into things, you know, that can help with that.
Then we can have like a really good approach.
And then first of all, for you, like the way I'm thinking is like overall, right, is going back to what we did before, which was we have the handbook, right, kind of how we work and things like that.
Then there is like...
We delivery section that is very, very simple on summarizing the eight weeks and things like that.
And then we had an SOP library.
So anything that require an SOP, there's a library and most of those have videos with it.
And then we had just the Notion thing that you duplicate.
And then the Notion template that you duplicate is what you duplicate for clients.
But there's a lot of sync blocks and the sync blocks are like just literally the latest and greatest instructions at that given point in time, you know.
And so we only manage like the sync blocks, right?
And so when you duplicate the client area in Notion, like a lot of that would already come with the instructions, like week one, week two, week three, four, week five, right?
Like here's what you do, here's like, and then like this is on the client delivery side.
And then what we're doing here is like overlaps quite a
Because we want every new employee to understand what happens here, right?
But over time, there might be like more things above and beyond.
But as far as like this project, there should be almost nothing that we work on that every single personal delivery side should not consume pretty much, right?
Like I can see somebody on the build side not needing to consume, but even then I'm like, why not?
You should consume everything to have the full context of the company, you know, as well.
Will, do you need me to prepare a script based on the videos so you can trim everything?
I think, you know, once we have kind of like, I think we're best off just from this point gathering as much as we can so we know.
Because I've got, there's a lot of stuff that's unpublished, you know, that I would love for you to like take a look at and consume.
I'll send you a link right after this meeting.
I have some summaries.
I

Loom only, but everything in Loom in that folder, I know like frame is a little bit easier, but just because like we're already paying for Loom, Loom has like some basic AI features and things like that that is just good enough, and everybody already has access to that, and a lot of people have already recorded Looms of themselves doing stuff too, so yeah.

I agree, yeah, and Loom will be where it all lives, but I just want to kind of give you everything, and then you can kind of look, because what I think is going to happen is we're going to discover, and Marcel already said it, that there's basically like multiple versions of the same thing, and there's the best version of it, and we need to filter out to find the best version, and then condense it down.

Because I think like even right now, where we're at with that one and a half hour video, I would venture to guess that that could be massively distilled down to maybe even a half an hour, and still be back.

And if we do go down the road of like having a transcript where we say, you know, here's in and out points and this is where we cut, you know, I think if we have a high level approach, it doesn't need to be super prescriptive, Marisol.
Like, I think that's maybe working harder, not smarter.
It's just kind of like high level.
What are the key themes that we're discussing that need to be included?
And then once I'm in the edit, that's like pretty easy to distill.
So for now.

And Marisol, like, I think like what just to kind of, I think summarize what Will's saying, but to emphasize the point here is that like, I think Will is like really, really familiar with the whole context of the company.
Cause like, we've been recording a lot of stuff, right?
Like, and he's been editing, reading transcripts.
he's probably listened to me more than anyone else in this company at this point.
I don't if that's a good thing or a bad thing.
But whereas you have.
A lot of context that he doesn't have around like how the strategy sprint actually works and kind of like the day-to-day.
And so I think it's a lot more valuable for you to focus on like the examples, the things that like are specific for like week one things we do, right?
Like flagging if we don't, if you don't find it anywhere, like for instance, like, hey, creating the company context or maybe it's like the writing guidelines.

Oh yeah, Marcel had that one loom or for instance, like remembering that like I used to do these stand-ups every single day with the strategy sprint teams and almost every day I would like pull something and then start doing it together.
Remember like, so, so like those are great to kind of try to find, you know, like finding those things, I think like Will wouldn't know to even know that that existed, you know, essentially.

And I think that's where you could be the most impactful in the short term and then Will can like take all the stuff he has plus stuff you're sending over and organizing and running through some of his prompts as well.
Yeah, totally agree.
Awesome.
Well, PingMe, this is my P0 to end the year and have everything kind of organized.
So when people come into the new year, we have a lot of the documentation, a lot of things, a lot of the enablement as well.
And then we can kind of focus on making sure people are actually reading, you know, and consuming.
I it's like, because I was going through with Ella, too, after we all sang to Marcel, and I was just like, just to give you an example, I was reading this 12-week thing that I had written.
And just so you all understand, like, the frustration that I felt, like, I spent probably, like, four hours on this.
let's go Thank
Probably more, this is like, you know, this was the source of truth, except we changed from a 90-day pilot to an eight-week sprint, and I wasn't hands-on with this anymore.
So instead of us taking this and going, hey, there's a lot here that is like how we approach every customer engagement, like own their goals, lighten their load, coach a supercharge.
It's fun, like celebrate small wins, daily communication, infinite mindset, like, you know, like a lot of these things, it's just like, cool, it's there.
And then instead, like some of the stuff we've done, I don't even know where it is right now, but well, this was like a decent version, right?
So then, like, I go into, like, even like this.
Stuff here, it's like, it's the delivery area, and it's like, there's a client onboarding process.
There's like, nothing about like, client onboarding that is easier to find, right?
Like, I was literally looking for it, and I was like, okay, where did it go?
Where's the source of truth?
And you see like, all this messiness here.
And I was like, this is crazy.
I was like, what are people consuming now?
You know?
You know, the process.
And there's nothing like, that I could find a mindset.
It's all like, needy-greedy.
Like, this is one task.
Do this one task, right?
And it's kind of like, you know, kind of missing the point a little bit, right?
It's like, okay, how do you do a content refresh?
It's like, what?
Like, where's the, like, where's the big picture?
the picture here?
here?
Like, what's coming in.
Looking at this, the content strategy generation, how to generate, you know, artifacts, like, so in the weeds, right?
And, like, you're clicking around, like, what is this?
Like, there's no context.
Like, we're just making people dig for it, you know?
And before, I had, like, literally, where did it go?
One thing.
It's just one doc, and anything that was important would link off to this doc, right?
And if there's videos explaining, like, link to things, make it easier.
Don't say, hey, go find a video on Fathom.
There's a video on Fathom.
Or don't give people the, like, two-hour Fathom where there's, like, a 30-minute snippet in there that's, like, important.
Don't make people kind of, like, connect the dots themselves.
It should just be, like, this is it.
This is how to think about it, right?
And so that's a lot of what drove, like, both the urgency as well as, like, the frustration.
I was like, oh, my God.
Like, all the good stuff got out.
Who archive this?
Why?
Why would we archive things that I worked so hard on and then replace it with nothing or way worse version or version with no context whatsoever?
So that's what we're trying to bring it back from the dead.
Yeah, makes so much sense.
Cool.
Awesome.

The team, I'm online the rest of the day today and then next Monday, and then I'll probably still be fairly responsive the rest of the time too, but I know with holiday schedules and stuff, hopefully we can make as much progress as we can, but totally understand it's holiday season.
No, no, this would be good, and we're already on our way, and now it's just kind of a matter of digging everything up on our end, but I do think we're on the same page.
Cool.
Awesome.
Thanks, team.
Talk soon.
Okay, Marcel, take it easy.
See y'all.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on the knowledge sharing project to consolidate company knowledge.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=43.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Problem:** Critical knowledge is fragmented across archived documents and uncurated videos, forcing new hires to "connect the dots" and hindering their ramp-up.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=869.0)
  - [**Goal:** Create a single source of truth by inventorying all content in Loom, transcribing it, and distilling \~30 hours of footage into a core 20-hour curriculum.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
  - [**Roles:** Marisol will source Strategy Sprint examples; Will will edit and organize content in Loom, guided by Marisol to ensure accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
  - [**Urgency:** This is Marcel's P0 (Priority Zero) for the year-end, aiming to have the new system ready for new hires in the new year.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=844.0)

## Topics

### The Knowledge Fragmentation Problem

  - [Critical knowledge from the last year is archived or lost, replaced by overly granular, task-focused SOPs that lack strategic context.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=869.0)
  - [**Example:** A 12-week delivery guide with core principles ("own their goals," "infinite mindset") was replaced by a fragmented process that forces users to dig for information.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=869.0)
  - [This forces new hires to "connect the dots" themselves, hindering their ramp-up.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=1030.0)
  - [**Example:** A superior video is unused while an old, less effective one is linked from job applications.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)

### The Solution: A Centralized Knowledge Hub

  - [**Objective:** Inventory all content to create a single source of truth, not to "boil the ocean."](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)
    1.  [**Consolidate:** Gather all content in one place.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)
    2.  [**Transcribe:** Process all video content.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)
    3.  [**Organize:** Add clear titles and chapters.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)
    4.  [**Curate:** Distill \~30 hours of footage into a core 20-hour curriculum.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
  - [**Platform:** Loom will be the central hub, leveraging its AI features and existing team familiarity.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)

### Roles & Responsibilities

  - [**Marisol:**](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [Source Strategy Sprint examples (e.g., Argument client kickoff, deep dives).](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=347.0)
      - [Flag key content (e.g., daily stand-up recordings) that Will wouldn't know existed.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [Review Will's edits to ensure they are "on message."](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [**Will:**](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [Download and organize all video content.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
      - [Edit videos into concise, single-angle clips.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [Decompose long videos (e.g., the 1.5-hour whiteboarding session) into digestible chapters.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=450.0)
      - [Use AI prompts to process content.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=501.0)

### Future State: A Structured System

  - [This project will feed into a structured knowledge system:](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**Handbook:** How we work.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**Delivery Section:** Summary of the 8-week sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**SOP Library:** Task-specific videos.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
      - [**Notion Client Template:** Uses sync blocks for up-to-date instructions.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Will:**](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [Download and organize all video content.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
      - [Send Marisol a link to the unpublished Loom folder.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
      - [Begin editing, running drafts past Marisol for review.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [**Marisol:**](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [Source and send Will Strategy Sprint examples.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=347.0)
      - [Review Will's edits for accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/R8Qqv4WuyZan-ZHSyR5YfygVgcs-b77n?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)

action_items.description: Send Loom folder link to Marisol; request review/flagging of best versions
